over.title: About
over.description:
I am a software developer who loves to build and quickly switch from an idea to something that works. I prefer learning by doing: picking up new technologies, applying them, and improving until it's right.

In projects, I take a structured approach and work step by step toward a working solution. I look beyond just whether something works technically. I also pay attention to how logical it is, how it's used, and whether it remains scalable.

When I work on something, I make sure it gets done. I think along about choices, work things out, and keep tweaking until it's set up well.

I enjoy working in a team and keep communication simple and clear. At the same time, I have no problem diving into something independently and figuring it out.

items.description:
During my internship at Bounsel in Valencia, I worked as a fullstack developer on Timbuzz, a desktop application built with Tauri and Nuxt.

I worked on both frontend and backend and was involved in components that directly impacted how users register their work and convert it into quotes. Because of this, I immediately saw what worked and what didn't, and I could improve it directly.

At Company 100, I worked with the CodeIgniter framework and an internal CMS on various web applications.

Here I learned to work within existing codebases and extend functionality without breaking the structure. I gained insight into how projects are set up and further developed in a professional environment.

items.school: Avans University of Applied Sciences Breda
items.degree: Bachelor's in Computer Science - propaedeutic certificate obtained
items.description:
I am currently studying HBO Computer Science at Avans University of Applied Sciences in Breda, where I obtained my propaedeutic certificate.

Within the program, I work a lot in group projects where we develop applications from start to finish. We start with an idea and work it out into a working product, including planning, development, and testing. In these projects, I learned how to work together towards a result and how to maintain an overview while everyone works on different parts.
Before that, I completed the MBO Software Developer program, where I laid my technical foundation.

hobby.title: Hobbies
hobby.description:
In my free time, I regularly hit the gym and focus on progression. You can also see this goal-oriented approach in how I handle other things.

In addition, I enjoy gaming and am very involved with music. I like trying out new things, whether it's a genre or something technical.

Even outside of projects, I often build or experiment with new technologies, just to see how something works and what you can do with it.
